findings.reasoning: R-04 is triggered only when an institution states a bonus in an advertisement (e.g., a deposit account bonus such as 'get a $300 bonus'). This page is an editorial tax-tips article about IRS interest rates on late payments and refunds. It discusses TurboTax product offers (Refund Advance loan, File Now Pay Later loan, Free Edition, Expert Full Service, Refer a Friend rewards) but none of these constitute a deposit account 'bonus' subject to bank bonus advertising disclosure requirements. The 'Refer a Friend' reward ($500 for 20 referrals) is the closest candidate, but this is a referral reward program, not a bank account opening bonus tied to APY/balance/time requirements as contemplated by the rule (which explicitly discusses APY, minimum balance to open/obtain, and time of bonus provision - hallmarks of deposit account bonus ads). No checking/savings account bonus advertisement appears anywhere on this page. Strongest counter-reading: one could argue the Refer a Friend reward or the Refund Advance/loan terms constitute a 'bonus' triggering the rule. However, R-04's five required disclosure elements (APY, time to obtain bonus, minimum balance to obtain bonus, minimum balance to open account, time bonus provided) are clearly deposit-account-specific concepts, and referral rewards or loan products are not the deposit-account bonus type this rule targets. Therefore the rule is not triggered on this page.

Rule R-04 is triggered only when the advertisement states a bonus on a deposit account (e.g., bank account opening bonus). This page discusses IRS interest rates, TurboTax product guarantees, pricing, loans, and refund-advance features, but there is no mention of any account-opening or deposit-related bonus, nor any phrasing like “get a $X bonus” tied to an account. Therefore the rule’s IF condition is not met, so the rule is not applicable to this page.

Reviewed the entire page, which is a tax tips article with product disclosures for TurboTax services and loan programs. There is no advertised bank/deposit account “bonus.” Mentions of Credit Karma Money banking services, early refund deposit, and referral rewards (“Maximum of $500 in total rewards for 20 referrals”) are not deposit account bonuses and do not state APY or similar. Rule R-04 (a Reg DD-style bonus disclosure rule) applies only when a bonus on a deposit account is advertised. Therefore, trigger is not met. Strongest counter-reading: the referral rewards line could be seen as a “bonus,” but it is not tied to a deposit account product and thus does not trigger APY/bonus disclosure requirements.
